cpu_universal_benchmark.scope: utils/cpu_universal_benchmark.py â€” systematic smoke benchmark across L1-L5 (one fixed prompt/tokens/threads, 5 env-var combinations)

results_bitnet_2b.interpretation: L3-L5 show no speedup over L1 because the model was NOT trained with ACDC/HRR/tropical architectures (P6 unvalidated). L5 -62% is the expected cost of FFT for head_dim=128. Cleanup overhead is negligible (8 iters أ— small d).

WHT_AVX2_g0_g3_swap.severity: HIGH â€” silently wrong for all 5 WHT test cases
WHT_AVX2_g0_g3_swap.root_cause: src/ggml-bitnet-wht.cpp:186-189 (before fix) â€” labels g0..g3 were inverted relative to unpack_i2s_block in the same file. Bits [7:6] represent group 0 (positions 0..31), not group 3. The library's own ggml_wht_verify (test [3]) also failed, indicating the bug was latent and undetected.

ACDC_1_over_n2_normalization.severity: HIGH â€” spec violation in central L3 kernel
ACDC_1_over_n2_normalization.root_cause: src/ggml-bitnet-fwht.cpp:291-303 (before fix) â€” acdc_forward_i8 applied a 1/nآ² factor (divided twice by n) that violated the spec in CLAUDE.md: 'Level 3 kernel: acdc_forward(x, d) = Hآ·(dâٹ™(Hآ·x)), UNNORMALIZED â€” no 1/nآ² factors. The diagonal d absorbs the scale when learned during training.'
ACDC_1_over_n2_normalization.test_coverage: test_acdc.cpp 5/5 PASS after fix; test [4] expectation also corrected (d*[k]=1/n for W=I, not 1)
